behaviors.description: The cron parser rejects invalid expressions (e.g., month 13, day 32, malformed syntax) with a descriptive error message.
behaviors.justification: Cron parsing is pure input validation with deterministic behavior. All parsing failures (invalid month/day values, malformed syntax) are directly testable by calling the parser function with invalid inputs and verifying rejection. No I/O, external dependencies, or infrastructure needed.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test the parser with invalid cron expressions: month 13, day 32, malformed syntax (missing fields, extra fields, invalid characters), and boundary cases. Verify each is rejected with a clear, descriptive error message. Also verify that well-formed cron expressions are accepted.

behaviors.description: The next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard cron expressions, including edge cases like 'last day of month' and 'nearest weekday.'
behaviors.justification: Next-run calculation is pure date/time arithmetic with deterministic output. All edge cases (leap-year February 29, month-end transitions, day-of-week computation) are testable by providing specific input dates and verifying computed timestamps. No I/O or external dependencies needed.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test the calculator with specific dates that trigger edge cases: leap-year February 29, month boundaries, last-day-of-month scenarios, nearest-weekday logic. Verify computed next-run timestamps match expected values. Include both forward and edge-case months.

behaviors.description: The overlap detector identifies when two schedules would fire within the configured minimum gap (e.g., 5 minutes) and reports the conflicting time windows.
behaviors.justification: Once schedule objects are available, overlap detection is pure logic comparing computed run times against a gap threshold. Deterministic and testable by providing schedule pairs and verifying correct conflict identification. No I/O or mocking of the core detection logic needed.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test the overlap detector with schedule pairs: some overlapping within the configured gap, some non-overlapping, and boundary cases exactly at the gap edge. Verify correct conflict detection, accurate time window reporting, absence of false positives, and handling of edge cases like simultaneous runs.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling produces correct run times across a DST transition — a job scheduled for 2:30 AM handles the spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity correctly.
behaviors.justification: Correct DST handling depends on the real timezone library's DST rules (pytz, zoneinfo, etc.). Mocking the timezone library would hide failures in DST-aware timestamp computation. Real timezone data is essential, though system time can be mocked to simulate DST transitions without waiting for them.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test using the real timezone library (e.g., pytz.timezone('America/New_York')). Mock the system time to be during spring-forward (2:00 AM doesn't exist, jumps to 3:00) and fall-back (2:00 AM occurs twice). Verify a job scheduled for 2:30 AM computes correct next-run timestamps, handles the gap correctly, resolves ambiguity appropriately, and does not crash or throw exceptions.

behaviors.description: When a scheduled job's computed next run time has already passed (e.g., system was down), the engine determines whether to skip or backfill based on the job's configured missed-run policy.
behaviors.justification: While skip/backfill decision logic is deterministic, the real failure boundary is whether the policy is actually applied during job execution. Mocking job scheduling would hide bugs where the policy decision is correct but the job executes (or fails to execute) contrary to that policy. Integration testing with real scheduling behavior and mocked time catches policy-application failures and race conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with system time mocked to be after a job's scheduled run time. Create jobs with skip-policy and backfill-policy configurations. Verify skip-policy prevents execution and backfill-policy queues/executes immediately. Include: jobs with no explicit policy (default behavior), multiple missed runs, boundary time conditions, and concurrent scheduling scenarios.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Concurrent execution race conditions (time check vs. actual scheduling) might require System-level subprocess testing for full edge-case coverage, but Integration should catch most policy-application failures.
